methodology_sections: 1. Lifecycle Integrity - State transitions, illegal states, predecessors
2. Monotonicity & Irreversibility - Increasing values, one-way transitions
3. Access & Permission Logic - Conditional access, role requirements
4. Time & Resource Integrity - Time-locks, resource bounds, expiry

invariants.description: At most one edge per mutualId group can ever be confirmed. If a rival is already confirmed, setConfirmedRival must revert.
invariants.attack_scenario: If two rival edges are both confirmed, two conflicting assertions could both be confirmed on the rollup, breaking the single valid chain invariant and potentially enabling double-spend or state corruption.
invariants.invariant_logic: forall mutualId: count(e in edges where e.mutualId() == mutualId AND e.status == Confirmed) <= 1
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_singleConfirmedRival(bytes32 mutualId) public view { assert(store.confirmedRivals[mutualId] == bytes32(0) || store.edges[store.confirmedRivals[mutualId]].status == EdgeStatus.Confirmed); }

invariants.description: Edge status transitions are strictly one-way: Pending → Confirmed. An edge can never transition from Confirmed back to Pending.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an edge's status could revert to Pending, an attacker could re-challenge a confirmed edge, re-open disputes, and delay or prevent honest assertions from being confirmed.
invariants.invariant_logic: forall edgeId: if edge.status == Confirmed at block N, then edge.status == Confirmed for all blocks M > N
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_edgeStatusIrreversible(bytes32 edgeId) public view { ChallengeEdge storage e = store.edges[edgeId]; if (e.confirmedAtBlock != 0) { assert(e.status == EdgeStatus.Confirmed); } }

invariants.description: The totalTimeUnrivaledCache for any edge must be monotonically non-decreasing. Once set, it can only increase or stay the same.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the timer cache could decrease, an attacker could manipulate it to prevent honest edges from ever accumulating enough unrivaled time to confirm, permanently blocking the honest assertion chain.
invariants.invariant_logic: forall edgeId: old(edge.totalTimeUnrivaledCache) <= edge.totalTimeUnrivaledCache
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_timerCacheMonotonic(bytes32 edgeId, uint64 previousCache) public view { assert(store.edges[edgeId].totalTimeUnrivaledCache >= previousCache); }

invariants.description: An edge can only be bisected once. Once lowerChildId and upperChildId are set, they cannot be changed. setChildren must revert if children already exist.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an edge could be re-bisected with different children, an attacker could replace honest child edges with malicious ones, redirecting the bisection game to produce an incorrect one-step proof resolution.
invariants.invariant_logic: forall edgeId: if edge.lowerChildId != 0 then edge.lowerChildId and edge.upperChildId are immutable
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_childrenSetOnce(bytes32 edgeId) public view { ChallengeEdge storage e = store.edges[edgeId]; if (e.lowerChildId != bytes32(0)) { assert(e.upperChildId != bytes32(0)); } }

invariants.description: Block-type layer zero edges can only be created for assertions that are Pending and have a sibling (i.e., the assertion's predecessor has at least two children). This prevents opening challenges on undisputed assertions.
invariants.attack_scenario: If block edges could be created for undisputed assertions, an attacker could grief honest validators by forcing unnecessary challenges and locking stakes, even when there is no real dispute.
invariants.invariant_logic: forall block edge e: assertionChain.isPending(e.claimId) == true AND assertionChain.getSecondChildCreationBlock(predecessor) > 0
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_blockEdgeOnlyDisputed(bytes32 edgeId) public view { ChallengeEdge storage e = store.edges[edgeId]; if (e.level == 0 && e.claimId != bytes32(0)) { assert(assertionChain.isPending(e.claimId)); } }

invariants.description: Non-block layer zero edges (BigStep/SmallStep) can only be created when the claimed edge has length one AND has at least one rival AND is Pending. This enforces the challenge decomposition hierarchy.
invariants.attack_scenario: If claiming edges without rivals were allowed, an attacker could skip bisection steps in the challenge game and directly create higher-level edges, potentially confirming a fraudulent assertion without properly bisecting to a one-step proof.
invariants.invariant_logic: forall non-block layer zero edge e: hasLengthOneRival(e.claimId) == true AND claimEdge.status == Pending
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_claimEdgeRequirements(bytes32 edgeId) public view { ChallengeEdge storage e = store.edges[edgeId]; if (e.level > 0 && e.claimId != bytes32(0)) { assert(store.hasLengthOneRival(e.claimId)); } }

invariants.description: confirmEdgeByOneStepProof can only be called on SmallStep-type edges of length exactly 1. This prevents confirmation bypass at inappropriate levels.
invariants.attack_scenario: If one-step proofs could be executed on Block or BigStep edges, or edges longer than 1, an attacker could confirm edges without properly bisecting down to the actual point of disagreement, confirming an incorrect state transition.
invariants.invariant_logic: confirmEdgeByOneStepProof(edgeId) requires: levelToType(edge.level) == SmallStep AND edge.length() == 1
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_ospOnlySmallStepLengthOne(bytes32 edgeId) public view { /* checked inside confirmEdgeByOneStepProof */ }

invariants.description: Stake refund is only possible for edges that are (1) Confirmed, (2) layer zero (have staker and claimId), and (3) not already refunded. This prevents double-refund attacks.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an edge could be refunded multiple times, the contract would be drained of stake tokens, preventing other legitimate refunds and causing insolvency.
invariants.invariant_logic: refundStake(edgeId) requires: edge.status == Confirmed AND edge.isLayerZero() AND edge.refunded == false; post: edge.refunded == true
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_noDoubleRefund(bytes32 edgeId) public view { ChallengeEdge storage e = store.edges[edgeId]; if (e.refunded) { assert(e.status == EdgeStatus.Confirmed && e.claimId != bytes32(0) && e.staker != address(0)); } }

invariants.description: Normal assertion confirmation requires prevAssertionHash == latestConfirmed(). Assertions must be confirmed sequentially along the chain. The winning edge's claimId must equal the assertionHash being confirmed.
invariants.attack_scenario: If assertions could be confirmed out of order or with a mismatched winning edge, an attacker could confirm a fraudulent assertion by referencing a different winning edge, breaking the integrity of the rollup state.
invariants.invariant_logic: confirmAssertion requires: prevAssertionHash == latestConfirmed() AND (prevAssertion has no second child OR (winningEdge.claimId == assertionHash AND winningEdge.status == Confirmed))
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_sequentialConfirmation(bytes32 assertionHash, bytes32 prevAssertionHash) public view { /* latestConfirmed must be prev */ assert(prevAssertionHash == latestConfirmed()); }

invariants.description: When a disputed assertion is confirmed (prevAssertion has second child), the winning edge's confirmedAtBlock + challengeGracePeriodBlocks must have passed. This grace period ensures the challenge result is observable before confirmation.
invariants.attack_scenario: Without the grace period, an attacker could confirm an assertion immediately after winning a challenge, before honest parties can observe and react, potentially enabling front-running of the confirmation with bridge withdrawals.
invariants.invariant_logic: if prevAssertion.secondChildBlock > 0: block.number >= winningEdge.confirmedAtBlock + challengeGracePeriodBlocks
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_gracePeriod(bytes32 winningEdgeId) public view { ChallengeEdge memory e = challengeManager.getEdge(winningEdgeId); if (e.confirmedAtBlock != 0) { assert(block.number >= e.confirmedAtBlock + challengeGracePeriodBlocks); } }

invariants.description: An edge can only be bisected if it is Pending and has at least one rival. Unrivaled edges cannot be bisected since their timer is ticking and bisection is unnecessary.
invariants.attack_scenario: If unrivaled edges could be bisected, an attacker could force honest validators to do unnecessary work bisecting uncontested edges, increasing costs and delaying confirmation.
invariants.invariant_logic: bisectEdge(edgeId) requires: edge.status == Pending AND hasRival(edgeId) == true
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_bisectOnlyRivaledPending(bytes32 edgeId) public view { ChallengeEdge storage e = store.edges[edgeId]; if (e.lowerChildId != bytes32(0)) { /* was bisected, so must have been rivaled at bisection time */ } }

invariants.description: timeUnrivaled for an edge is frozen once it gets a rival. After a rival is created, the edge's unrivaled time is fixed at (rivalCreatedAtBlock - edgeCreatedAtBlock) or 0, and can never increase again.
invariants.attack_scenario: If timeUnrivaled could increase after a rival appeared, an attacker could create and then somehow remove rivals to inflate an edge's timer, enabling premature confirmation of a fraudulent edge.
invariants.invariant_logic: if hasRival(edgeId): timeUnrivaled(edgeId) == max(0, firstRival.createdAtBlock - edge.createdAtBlock) [constant]
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_timeUnrivaledFrozen(bytes32 edgeId) public view { if (store.hasRival(edgeId)) { /* timeUnrivaled is deterministic from stored block numbers */ } }

invariants.description: updateTimerCacheByClaim requires the claiming edge's originId to match the claimed edge's mutualId, and the claiming edge to be exactly one level above the claimed edge. This ensures cross-level timer inheritance follows the correct hierarchy.
invariants.attack_scenario: If timer inheritance could cross arbitrary levels or use unrelated edges, an attacker could inflate a lower-level edge's timer by referencing an unrelated high-timer edge, enabling premature confirmation.
invariants.invariant_logic: updateTimerCacheByClaim(edgeId, claimingEdgeId): store.edges[edgeId].mutualId() == store.edges[claimingEdgeId].originId AND store.edges[claimingEdgeId].level == store.edges[edgeId].level + 1
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_claimLinkValid(bytes32 edgeId, bytes32 claimingEdgeId) public view { assert(store.edges[edgeId].mutualId() == store.edges[claimingEdgeId].originId); }

invariants.description: fastConfirmAssertion bypasses the prev==latestConfirmed and challenge winner checks. It can only be called by anyTrustFastConfirmer. If anyTrustFastConfirmer is compromised or misconfigured, arbitrary pending assertions can be confirmed out of order.
invariants.attack_scenario: A compromised or malicious fast confirmer could confirm a fraudulent assertion, skipping all challenge resolution. This would allow invalid state transitions to be finalized, enabling theft of bridged funds.
invariants.invariant_logic: fastConfirmAssertion caller == anyTrustFastConfirmer; no prevAssertionHash == latestConfirmed check; _latestConfirmed can jump to any pending assertion
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_fastConfirmOnlyTrusted() public view { /* msg.sender == anyTrustFastConfirmer is the sole guard */ }

invariants.description: confirmEdgeByTime can only be called on layer zero edges (edges with non-zero claimId and staker). Non-layer-zero edges (created by bisection) must accumulate time through their parents and cannot be directly confirmed by time.
invariants.attack_scenario: If non-layer-zero child edges could be confirmed by time directly, an attacker could skip the full bisection game by confirming a child edge without properly resolving the dispute down to a one-step proof.
invariants.invariant_logic: confirmEdgeByTime(edgeId) requires: edge.isLayerZero() == true (edge.claimId != 0 AND edge.staker != address(0))
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_confirmByTimeOnlyLayerZero(bytes32 edgeId) public view { ChallengeEdge storage e = store.edges[edgeId]; if (e.status == EdgeStatus.Confirmed && e.confirmedAtBlock != 0) { /* could have been confirmed by time or OSP */ } }

invariants.description: getPrevAssertionHash traverses levels using firstRivals mapping. If any intermediate firstRivals entry equals UNRIVALED (no rival exists), the traversal would fail because UNRIVALED is not a valid edge ID. This requires that all intermediate claimed edges have rivals.
invariants.attack_scenario: If getPrevAssertionHash is called for an edge whose level chain has an intermediate edge without rivals (theoretically impossible since edges require hasLengthOneRival), the function would revert, blocking one-step proof confirmation.
invariants.invariant_logic: forall edgeId used in confirmEdgeByOneStepProof: traversing from edge up through levels via firstRivals always yields valid edge IDs (never UNRIVALED)
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_traversalValid(bytes32 edgeId) public view { bytes32 id = edgeId; ChallengeEdge storage e = store.edges[id]; while (e.level > 0) { bytes32 fr = store.firstRivals[e.originId]; assert(fr != bytes32(0) && fr != UNRIVALED); id = fr; e = store.edges[id]; } }

invariants.description: A staker can only create a new assertion if their latestStakedAssertion is either the prev of the new assertion OR their latestStakedAssertion has a child. This prevents staking on two divergent branches simultaneously.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a staker could stake on multiple branches without restriction, they could amplify their influence across competing assertion chains, undermining the economic security of the stake-based challenge system.
invariants.invariant_logic: stakeOnNewAssertion requires: latestStakedAssertion(sender) == prevAssertion OR getAssertionStorage(latestStakedAssertion(sender)).firstChildBlock > 0
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_singleBranchStake(address staker) public view { bytes32 last = latestStakedAssertion(staker); assert(last == latestConfirmed() || getAssertionStorage(last).firstChildBlock > 0 || !isStaked(staker)); }

invariants.description: Assertion status transitions are strictly NoAssertion → Pending → Confirmed. An assertion can never transition backwards. Once confirmed, latestConfirmed advances and can never regress to a previous assertion.
invariants.attack_scenario: If assertion status could revert or latestConfirmed could point to an earlier assertion, confirmed L2 state could be rolled back, enabling double-spend attacks on the bridge.
invariants.invariant_logic: forall assertionHash: if status == Confirmed at block N, then status == Confirmed for all M > N; _latestConfirmed never points to an ancestor of its current value via normal confirmation
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_assertionStatusIrreversible(bytes32 assertionHash) public view { AssertionNode storage a = getAssertionStorage(assertionHash); if (a.status == AssertionStatus.Confirmed) { assert(a.createdAtBlock > 0); } }

invariants.description: When validator whitelist is enabled, each whitelisted account can create at most one layer zero edge per mutualId. This prevents a single validator from creating rival edges to grief the system.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a single whitelisted validator could create multiple rival layer zero edges, they could force other honest validators to respond to multiple challenges, draining their resources and stakes disproportionately.
invariants.invariant_logic: if whitelistEnabled: forall (account, mutualId) pair, at most one layer zero edge created by account with that mutualId
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_singleRivalPerValidator(address account, bytes32 mutualId) public view { /* hasMadeLayerZeroRival[account][mutualId] set to true on first creation, second attempt reverts */ }

invariants.description: Timer cache propagation must happen bottom-up: children's caches must be updated before parent's cache can reflect inherited time. confirmEdgeByTime reads children's caches which default to 0, so if propagation is incomplete, confirmed time will be underestimated but never overestimated.
invariants.attack_scenario: Not a direct attack vector but a liveness concern: if an honest validator fails to propagate timer caches from SmallStep leaves through BigStep to Block level before calling confirmEdgeByTime, the total accumulated time will be underestimated, potentially delaying honest assertion confirmation beyond expected timelines.
invariants.invariant_logic: edge.totalTimeUnrivaledCache <= timeUnrivaled(edge) + min(children's actual accumulated time); cache is conservative lower bound
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_cacheIsLowerBound(bytes32 edgeId) public view { uint256 actual = store.timeUnrivaledTotal(edgeId); assert(store.edges[edgeId].totalTimeUnrivaledCache <= actual); }
